claim: MHRA granted Great Britain marketing authorisation for lecanemab (Leqembi) in early Alzheimer's disease (22 Aug 2024) but restricted it to ApoE4 non-carriers and heterozygotes, excluding e4 homozygotes.
divergence_vs_fda: FDA granted traditional approval (Jul 2023) without an upfront ApoE4 genotype restriction; MHRA restricted to ApoE4 non-carriers/heterozygotes from authorisation.
divergence_implication: An anti-amyloid programme may face a narrower ex-US label gated on ApoE4 genotype even where the FDA approves broadly; genotype-stratified ARIA risk drives the restriction.
quote: Lecanemab is approved to treat adults in the early stages of Alzheimer's disease who have one or no copies of the apolipoprotein E4 gene (ApoE4).
tier_note: MHRA (gov.uk) ex-US national-regulator primary; T1 under the extended gate allowlist (PR #31; dev/HELP.md RESOLVED 2026-06-24).

claim: MHRA granted Great Britain marketing authorisation for donanemab (Kisunla) in early Alzheimer's disease (23 Oct 2024), restricted to patients with one or no copies of ApoE4 (excluding e4 homozygotes).
divergence_vs_fda: FDA approved donanemab (Jul 2024) without an upfront ApoE4 genotype gate; MHRA limited use to non-homozygotes and contraindicated anticoagulant users.
divergence_implication: Confirms a consistent ex-US pattern: UK/EU regulators gate anti-amyloid mAbs on ApoE4 genotype to manage ARIA, a constraint absent from the initial US label.
quote: Donanemab is approved to treat adults in the early stages of Alzheimer's disease who have one or no copies of the apolipoprotein E4 gene (ApoE4).
tier_note: MHRA (gov.uk) ex-US national-regulator primary; T1 under the extended gate allowlist (PR #31; dev/HELP.md RESOLVED 2026-06-24).

claim: NICE final draft guidance (19 Jun 2025) did not recommend lecanemab or donanemab for NHS use, judging the benefit too small to justify the cost - a reimbursement (market-access) divergence even though MHRA had licensed both.
divergence_vs_fda: Both are FDA-approved and MHRA-licensed; NICE, the HTA body, declined NHS funding on cost-effectiveness - a regulator-approved-but-not-reimbursed split.
divergence_implication: Marketing authorisation is not market access: an anti-amyloid programme can clear ex-US regulators yet be blocked at HTA/reimbursement, the binding commercial gate in single-payer systems.
quote: The committee's conclusion in final draft guidance published today remains that neither drug can be recommended for treating mild cognitive impairment or mild dementia caused by Alzheimer's disease.

claim: The EMA's CHMP adopted a negative opinion for eteplirsen (Exondys) in Duchenne muscular dystrophy (1 Jun 2018; refusal confirmed on re-examination Sep 2018), whereas the FDA had granted accelerated approval in Sept 2016 on the dystrophin surrogate.
divergence_vs_fda: FDA accelerated-approved eteplirsen (Sep 2016) accepting increased dystrophin as a surrogate reasonably likely to predict benefit; EMA CHMP adopted a negative opinion (2018), unconvinced the dystrophin increase showed clinical benefit.
divergence_implication: Acceptance of a biochemical surrogate (dystrophin) is regulator-specific: a US accelerated approval on a surrogate does not predict EMA acceptance, which here demanded functional-endpoint evidence.
quote: the Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) of the European Medicines Agency (EMA), has adopted a negative opinion for EXONDYS (eteplirsen), as expected. EXONDYS is designed to treat patients with Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) amenable to skipping exon 51 of the dystrophin gene.
tier_note: Sponsor secondary (announces the EMA action). EMA-primary (EPAR refusal) not fetched this pass - EMA website search was down 2026-06-24; flagged in manifest known-gaps for T1 upgrade.

claim: The FDA issued a Refuse to File letter for ataluren (Translarna) in nonsense-mutation DMD (22 Feb 2016), stating the NDA was not sufficiently complete for review - while the EMA had granted conditional marketing authorisation in 2014. A reverse divergence (EU-yes / US-no).
regulator: FDA (Refuse to File) vs EMA (conditional MA 2014)
divergence_vs_fda: EMA granted conditional marketing authorisation in 2014; the FDA refused to file the NDA in 2016 (insufficiently complete) and never approved it - ex-US approval preceding and diverging from a US refusal.
divergence_implication: Divergence runs both ways: a drug can be EU-authorised yet fail even to be filed by the FDA. EU conditional-approval precedent does not lower the US substantial-evidence/filing bar.
quote: it received yesterday evening a Refuse to File letter from the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regarding PTC's New Drug Application (NDA) for Translarna (ataluren), an oral, first-in-class, protein restoration therapy for the treatment of nonsense mutation Duchenne muscular dystrophy (nmDMD).
tier_note: Quote/url support the FDA Refuse-to-File (US action). The EMA 2014 conditional MA is the divergence framing; EMA-primary not fetched (EMA site down 2026-06-24) - manifest known-gap.

claim: The EMA decided not to authorise aducanumab (Aduhelm) for early Alzheimer's disease (17 Dec 2021; Biogen withdrew on re-examination Apr 2022), while the FDA had granted accelerated approval in June 2021 on the amyloid surrogate - the flagship FDA/EMA Alzheimer's divergence.
divergence_vs_fda: FDA granted accelerated approval (Jun 2021) on amyloid lowering as a surrogate; EMA found insufficient evidence that amyloid reduction predicts clinical benefit and refused - opposite calls on the same trials.
divergence_implication: A surrogate-based US accelerated approval can be wholly rejected ex-US; EMA required demonstrated clinical benefit, not amyloid reduction alone. Strongest precedent that amyloid-surrogate approvals do not travel.
quote: On 17 December 2021, the European Medicines Agency (EMA), in a decision closely watched by the European dementia community, decided not to authorise aducanumab for the treatment of mild cognitive impairment due to Alzheimer's disease and mild Alzheimer's dementia.
tier_note: NGO secondary (reports the EMA decision faithfully). EMA-primary (refusal Q&A) not fetched - EMA site search down 2026-06-24; manifest known-gap for T1 upgrade.

claim: ARIA risk with lecanemab rises sharply with APOE e4 allele dose (carriers 16.8%, e4 homozygotes 34.5% ARIA-E), the biomedical basis for restricting lecanemab to non-carriers/heterozygotes ex-US.
class_basis: APOE e4 allele dose -> ARIA-E risk (CLARITY AD Core+OLE, n=1612 lecanemab-dosed)
divergence_implication: Grounds why MHRA/EMA gate the anti-amyloid mAbs on ApoE4 genotype (cards: MHRA lecanemab/donanemab, EMA aducanumab).
quote: ARIA-E generally occurred within 3-6 months of treatment, was more common in ApoE e4 carriers (16.8%) and most common in ApoE e4 homozygous participants (34.5%).

claim: ARIA with donanemab is frequent and rises with APOE e4 allele number (ARIA-E 24.4% vs placebo 1.9%; ARIA-H 31.3% vs 13.0%), the biomedical basis for the ex-US ApoE4-gated donanemab labels.
class_basis: APOE e4 allele number -> ARIA-E risk (TRAILBLAZER-ALZ & ALZ 2 secondary analysis, n=3030)
divergence_implication: Supports the regulator-specific ApoE4 restriction; APOE e4 status is an independent ARIA risk factor regulators used to narrow the label.
quote: Risk analysis demonstrated independent associations between ARIA-E and 6 baseline variables, including increased risk with APOE e4 allele number, greater number of microhemorrhages, presence of cortical superficial siderosis, higher amyloid plaque, and elevated mean arterial pressure.

claim: Systemic eteplirsen (AVI-4658) produced a dose-dependent but modest and variable dystrophin increase (responders ~8.9% to 16.4% of normal) - the biochemical surrogate the FDA accepted for accelerated approval but the EMA found insufficient for clinical benefit.
class_basis: Exon-51-skipping morpholino ASO -> dose-dependent but modest dystrophin restoration (Cirak phase 2, n=19)
divergence_implication: Quantifies the surrogate at the heart of the eteplirsen FDA-yes/EMA-no divergence: a real but small dystrophin gain whose link to function was unproven - accepted by FDA, rejected by EMA.
quote: AVI-4658 induced exon 51 skipping in all cohorts and new dystrophin protein expression in a significant dose-dependent (p=0.0203), but variable, manner in boys from cohort 3 (dose 2 mg/kg) onwards.
